content.rule: The owner shouldn't need to believe you. They should be able to check. Every delivery must come with verifiable evidence. If you don't have evidence, the work is not done.
content.why: Claude has a strong tendency to say 'done, passed, works' without verifying. It's structural optimism from training. 'Said it passed' and 'passed' are different things, and the owner has been burned trusting the first.
how_to_apply: Dev: output of build, test, lint, type check pasted (not 'I ran it and it passed'). Screenshot for UI. Grep of actual callsite to prove the right code path was touched
Design: screenshot or link to final artboard. Cross-reference to design system tokens respected
Marketing: full draft pasted. Not 'I wrote it and it's good'
Research: sources cited with URL, author, date. Raw data or screenshot of source
Writing: final text pasted. Specific excerpt when edit was point-scoped
Product (PM): link to PRD/RDD. Traceable decisions
Discipline-specific self-QA checklists live in the Manager's agent file — this rule requires evidence exists, the Manager defines what counts as good evidence
anti_patterns: 'Tested and it works' without describing what was tested
'Researched the market' without a list of sources
'Fixed the bug' without explaining root cause
content.responsibility: All agents. Leo rejects syntheses from Managers that don't comply. Managers reject deliveries from specialists that don't comply. Cascade of rigor.
